content:
This paper builds a general mathematical and algorithmic theory for balloon-twisting structures,
from balloon animals to balloon polyhedra, by modeling their underlying graphs (edge skeleta). In
particular, we give algorithms to find the fewest balloons that can make exactly a desired graph or, using
fewer balloons but allowing repeated traversal or shortcuts, the minimum total length needed by a given
number of balloons. In contrast, we show NP-completeness of determining whether such an optimal
construction is possible with balloons of equal length.
